comment: Delta-based generation for Image Captioning: Improve only the trainable CrossModalBridge of the Blip2Fast-style captioning model.
prompt: Task: Improve the trainable feature-fusion bridge of a Blip2Fast-style image captioning model.
- Gating: gate = torch.sigmoid(self.g(x)); return x + gate * self.f(x).
- view(), reshape(), flatten(), permute(), transpose() — these break (B,32,768) shape.
- nn.LayerNorm([32, 768]) or nn.LayerNorm([768, 32]) — use nn.LayerNorm(768) ONLY.
- nn.self.Linear, nn.self.LayerNorm — use nn.Linear, nn.LayerNorm (NO nn.self).
- uninitialized self.dim — NEVER use self.dim. ALWAYS use 768 for dimensions.
- passing nn.Dropout(...) as dropout argument — pass float dropout probability directly.
- changing embedding dimension — bridge input and output MUST remain 768. Do not project to 256 or 512.
- nn.Conv2d, nn.Conv1d, nn.AdaptiveAvgPool — wrong for sequence data.
- prm.get('key') without a default value — ALWAYS write prm.get('key', default_value).
- Any undefined class: FlashAttentionLayer, AdapterBlock, ResidualBottleneck, etc.
- torch.bmm or torch.einsum — use nn.MultiheadAttention or nn.Linear instead.
- captions parameter — IGNORE it entirely, just use: def forward(self, x, captions=None): return ...
=== EXAMPLE OF FORBIDDEN CODE (DO NOT DO THIS) ===
# WRONG: x = x.view(B, -1)  → kills seq dimension
# WRONG: self.bn = int(prm.get('bn'))  → None crash if key missing
# WRONG: nn.TransformerEncoderLayer(..., dropout=self.dropout) → must be float
def forward(self, x, captions=None):  # ignore captions
# x shape: (B, 32, 768) — preserve this in output!
[ ] Every prm.get() call has a default value.
[ ] forward() returns a tensor of shape (B, 32, 768).
[ ] Zero-init the last Linear layer for training stability.
[ ] Use residual connection: return x + self.delta(x).
